Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Further Responses
Off Calendar Oct-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ethan Jacobs, Esq. 415-200-4984 of Holland Law for Plaintiff FORCE OF NATURE, INC., PETER KLAPPER.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF FORCE OF NATURE, INC., PETER KLAPPER's Motion To Compel Further Responses.

The Court will grant the motion to compel as to:
(1) Form Interrogatories 12.2 and 12.3. Defendants shall clarify the privilege relied upon; if the same includes qualified work product privilege, defendants shall make the make the foundational showing required by Coito v. Superior Court or answer the Interrogatory without objection.
(2) Form Interrogatories 14.1 and 14.2. Defendants shall clarify their present contentions and any foreseeable contentions as to violations of statutes, ordinances or regulations or citations therefor. Defendants shall not be required to identify any or all persons whom they do not contend violated statutes, ordinances or regulations.
(3) Form Interrogatory 17.1 as to Requests for Admission Nos. 13, 14, and 15. Defendants shall respond directly to the inquiry posed and may provide such further information as is necessary for a complete response.
(4) Responses to Requests for Admission Nos. 1-15. The General Objections will be stricken.
Sanctions are denied.
The parties are encouraged, particularly in the case of this complex dispute, to tailor their discovery to the matters at issue, to likewise tailor their objections to demonstrably improper demands and to endeavor to resolve discovery disputes by meeting and conferring without the assistance of the Court.

Order is signed.(see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
